title: AdoIntegrationConfig
description: Schema for Azure DevOps integration configuration declared in the ado_integration section of project.yaml. Defines organization, authentication, sync behaviour, state/type/field mappings, and filtering rules for GitHub-to-ADO work item synchronisation.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of this configuration schema.

enabled.description: Whether the ADO integration is active. Set to false to disable sync without removing configuration.

project.description: ADO project name where work items are created and synced.

auth_method.description: Authentication method for the ADO REST API. PAT uses a personal access token, service_principal uses OAuth2 client credentials, managed_identity uses Azure Managed Identity.

auth_secret_name.description: Name of the GitHub Actions secret or environment variable containing the authentication credential.

sync.description: Synchronisation behaviour configuration controlling direction, automation, and conflict resolution.

direction.description: Sync direction. 'bidirectional' syncs changes in both directions, 'github_to_ado' pushes GitHub issue changes to ADO, 'ado_to_github' pulls ADO work item changes to GitHub, 'disabled' turns off sync entirely.

auto_create.description: Automatically create ADO work items when new GitHub issues are opened.

auto_close.description: Automatically close/resolve ADO work items when the linked GitHub issue is closed.

grace_period_seconds.description: Seconds to wait after a change event before syncing, to debounce rapid successive updates.

conflict_strategy.description: Strategy for resolving conflicting updates. 'last_write' uses the most recent timestamp, 'field_ownership' assigns each field to one system, 'github_wins' always prefers GitHub, 'ado_wins' always prefers ADO.

sync_comments.description: Sync comments between GitHub issues and ADO work items. ADO comments are HTML-only.

sync_attachments.description: Sync file attachments between GitHub issues and ADO work items.

comment_prefix.description: Prefix required on comments for them to be synced between systems. Only comments starting with this prefix are eligible for cross-system sync.

state_mapping.description: Maps GitHub issue states to ADO work item states. Keys are GitHub states (e.g., 'open', 'closed') or compound states (e.g., 'open+ado:active'). Values are ADO state names.

type_mapping.description: Maps GitHub issue type labels to ADO work item types. The 'default' key specifies the fallback type when no label matches.

field_mapping.description: Configures how GitHub issue metadata maps to ADO work item fields.

area_path.description: Default ADO area path for new work items. Empty string uses the project root.

iteration_path.description: Default ADO iteration path. '@CurrentIteration' uses the current sprint.

priority_labels.description: Maps GitHub label names to ADO priority values (1=Critical, 2=High, 3=Medium, 4=Low).

user_mapping.description: Maps GitHub usernames to ADO user emails for assignee sync. Keys are GitHub usernames, values are ADO user email addresses.

filters.description: Controls which GitHub issues are eligible for ADO sync.

include_labels.description: Only sync issues with at least one of these labels. Empty array means all issues are eligible.

exclude_labels.description: Never sync issues with any of these labels.

ado_area_path_filter.description: Only sync ADO work items under this area path (for ado_to_github direction). Empty string means no filtering.

area_path_mapping.description: Maps GitHub label suffixes (from 'area:*' labels) to ADO area paths. Keys are the label suffix (e.g., 'Team A' for label 'area:Team A'), values are full ADO area paths (e.g., 'Project\Team A').

iteration_mapping.description: Maps GitHub milestone titles to ADO iteration paths. Keys are milestone titles, values are full ADO iteration paths. The special value '@CurrentIteration' maps to the current ADO sprint.
